reasoning: Based on recent FRED data, the Nominal Broad US Dollar Index has shown a steady decline throughout 2025, falling from a high near 129 in early 2025 to around 120 by mid-December 2025. The general macroeconomic consensus for 2026 heavily favors a "moderate softening" of the USD due to anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts and narrowing global interest-rate differentials. Consequently, the probability of the index increasing relative to its late-December level is lower than 50%. Obeying the strict instruction that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon (meaning probabilities for more distant dates must be closer to 0.5), I have set the short-term probability (Dec 28) furthest from 0.5 to reflect the immediate strong downward momentum, and increased the probabilities closer to 0.5 for the 1-month and 3-month horizons to reflect greater uncertainty over longer periods.
